ops.title: ship push race root cause: TOCTOU + bare git lane + conflicted-bookmark trap
ops.body:
A `ccx vcs ship` failed with a raw non-fast-forward rejection when a concurrent session advanced origin/main mid-ship, despite docs claiming in-call fetch+rebase handling. Root cause was three stacked gaps:

1. **jj lane TOCTOU**: `shipPushJJ` fetched, ancestor-checked, rebased, moved the bookmark, then pushed — several subprocesses between fetch and push with no rejection classification and no retry. A push is a compare-and-swap; the window cannot be closed client-side, only absorbed by re-entering fetch→rebase→push on a classified rejection.
2. **git lane had no protection at all**: `shipPushGit` was a bare `git push` — no fetch, no rebase. The documented fetch-first behavior was jj-only.
3. **Conflicted-bookmark trap**: a failed jj push left the local bookmark already advanced (move precedes push), so even the documented manual re-run bounced — the rerun's fetch saw local-moved + remote-moved, three-way-merged the ref into a conflicted bookmark, and ship's own heads>1 check refused. Recovery therefore must `jj op restore` to the attempt-entry op *before* re-fetching.

Fix (2026-07-22): typed `pushRejectedError` contract — a lane returns it only after rolling local state back to attempt entry — with a 3-attempt shared retry loop; git lane gains fetch-first + `rebase --autostash` parity; `--amend` rejections refuse on both backends (a refreshed force-with-lease would silently overwrite the concurrent push — any fetch on the amend path turns the lease into a rubber stamp). Classifiers match git's per-ref reason tokens `(non-fast-forward)`/`(fetch first)`/`(stale info)` and jj's "unexpectedly moved" — never `hint:` lines (advice.* can silence them), never jj's ambiguous terminal "Failed to push some bookmarks" (also printed on permission failures).
